items.question_text: Complete: Assign the runway/s most nearly aligned with the wind when _____ knots or more, or the calm wind runway when less than 5 knots.
items.explanation: The 5-knot threshold separates the wind-aligned runway rule from the calm-wind runway rule.

items.question_text: Who determines which runways are designated RUNWAY IN USE/ACTIVE RUNWAY/DUTY RUNWAY?
items.explanation: The ATCT supervisor or CIC has this responsibility. It is not a decision made by individual local controllers or by approach control.

choices.text: The approach controller based on the inbound flow rate.
Any certified local controller with current weather data.
The airport manager based on published noise abatement procedures.

items.question_text: Complete: A designated STOL runway may be assigned only when requested by the pilot or as specified in a _____ with an aircraft operator.
items.explanation: STOL runway assignment is restricted to pilot request or LOA provisions. The controller cannot assign a STOL runway independently.

items.question_text: Under 3-5-2, what must the controller issue if a pilot requests the measured length of a STOL runway?
items.explanation: The paragraph says to issue the measured STOL runway length if the pilot requests it. It's an on-request information item, not automatically provided.

choices.text: The available landing distance from the touchdown zone marker only.
Conventional runway length only, because STOL runways use the same measurement standard as regular runways.
No length information — the paragraph only authorizes runway assignment, not length reporting.

items.question_text: Complete: When authorizing use of runways and a tailwind component exists, always state both wind _____ and velocity.
items.explanation: The tailwind authorization must include both direction and velocity — these are mandatory elements when a tailwind exists.

items.question_text: A controller authorizes a pilot to use a runway with a tailwind. The controller says 'Wind calm.' Is this appropriate under 3-5-3?
items.explanation: The paragraph says 'The wind may be described as calm when appropriate.' A tailwind exists means the wind is not calm — it has a measurable direction and velocity. The controller must state both when a tailwind component exists. 'Calm' is only appropriate when the wind actually is calm.

choices.text: Yes — calm can be used instead of direction/velocity whenever the controller prefers brevity.
No — when a tailwind component exists, both wind direction and velocity must be stated; calm is only appropriate when the wind is actually calm.
Yes but only if the tailwind component is less than 5 knots.
No — calm may only be used for headwind runway assignments, never for tailwind authorizations.
